title: systemd --user session broken in fedora:31 container
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
"Interactive" sessions broken for users inside of Fedora 31 NON-privileged systemd container:
  - systemd-logind doesn't start
  - any user auth takes ~25 seconds, including su, sudo, etc

This might be a fedora31/systemd-243 issue, since on the same host, fedora30 container works as expected (since https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3459 was fixed)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. $ sudo podman rm -vf sysuser; sudo podman run -it -d --name sysuser fedora:31 /sbin/init
2. $ sudo podman exec -it sysuser bash -c "systemctl unmask systemd-logind --now; useradd user; systemctl restart systemd-logind; systemctl status -l --all systemd-logind"
```
Removed /etc/systemd/system/systemd-logind.service.
Job for systemd-logind.service failed because the control process exited with error code.
See "systemctl status systemd-logind.service" and "journalctl -xe" for details.
● systemd-logind.service - Login Service
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/systemd-logind.service; static; vendor preset: disabled)
   Active: activating (start) since Tue 2019-11-05 06:51:55 UTC; 4ms ago
     Docs: man:systemd-logind.service(8)
           man:logind.conf(5)
           https://www.freedesktop.org/wiki/Software/systemd/logind
           https://www.freedesktop.org/wiki/Software/systemd/multiseat
  Process: 66 ExecStartPre=/sbin/modprobe -abq drm (code=exited, status=226/NAMESPACE)
 Main PID: 67 ((d-logind))
   CGroup: /machine.slice/libpod-a4daeae644a6e856663ff0bf2dc9626f68cb9fc43b3e966d5afb2d6704e2216a.scope/system.slice/systemd-logind.service

Nov 05 06:51:55 a4daeae644a6 systemd[1]: Starting Login Service...
Nov 05 06:51:55 a4daeae644a6 systemd[1]: systemd-logind.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=226/NAMESPACE
Nov 05 06:51:55 a4daeae644a6 systemd[1]: systemd-logind.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Nov 05 06:51:55 a4daeae644a6 systemd[1]: Failed to start Login Service.
Nov 05 06:51:55 a4daeae644a6 systemd[1]: systemd-logind.service: Service has no hold-off time (RestartSec=0), scheduling restart.
Nov 05 06:51:55 a4daeae644a6 systemd[1]: systemd-logind.service: Scheduled restart job, restart counter is at 3.
Nov 05 06:51:55 a4daeae644a6 systemd[1]: Stopped Login Service.
Error: non zero exit code: 3: OCI runtime error
```

3. $ time sudo podman exec -it sysuser su - user -c 'bash -c "id"'
```
uid=1000(user) gid=1000(user) groups=1000(user)

real    0m25.401s
user    0m0.079s
sys     0m0.053s
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.10
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

```

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical

comments.body:
that looks like a change in systemd, I can confirm `fedora:30` works fine for me as well on a Fedora 31 host with cgroups v2 enabled while `fedora:31` fails with the same error message:

```
Nov 05 11:05:26 68720cac239e systemd[57]: systemd-logind.service: Failed at step NAMESPACE spawning /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-logind: Operation not supported

```

Could you file an issue with systemd?
thanks!  Since there is systemd issue to track the regression, I am going to close this.
